quick_summary: Bootstrap is a CSS framework providing a responsive 12-column grid system, pre-built UI components, and utility classes. It matters for interviews because it's still the most-used CSS framework in enterprise environments, and understanding its grid system demonstrates you can build responsive layouts without custom CSS.

detailed_content.explanation:
Bootstrap's grid system uses a 12-column layout with flexbox. The `.container`, `.row`, and `.col-*` classes create responsive layouts without writing custom CSS. Columns automatically resize based on breakpoint classes: `.col-sm`, `.col-md`, `.col-lg`, `.col-xl`. The grid uses mobile-first media queries — `col-md-6` means the column is full-width below 768px and half-width above.

Think of the grid as a 12-slot shelf. A `.col-md-4` takes 4 slots (one-third), `.col-md-6` takes 6 slots (half), `.col-md-12` takes all 12 (full width). You can nest rows inside columns for complex layouts. Offset classes (`offset-md-2`) add empty columns before content. The key insight: design for mobile first, then add breakpoint classes for larger screens.
detailed_content.example:
```html
Sidebar (full width on mobile, 1/3 on desktop)
Main Content (stacked on mobile, 5/12 on desktop)
Aside (stacked on mobile, 1/4 on desktop)
Status: Active
```
detailed_content.key_takeaway: Bootstrap's grid is 12 columns + mobile-first breakpoints — `col-md-6` means full-width on mobile, half-width on desktop. Design mobile first, then add breakpoint classes upward.
detailed_content.try_it: Build a responsive pricing page with 3 cards that stack vertically on mobile and sit side-by-side on desktop. Use Bootstrap's card component, grid classes, and utility classes for spacing. No custom CSS allowed.
detailed_content.complexity: Time: O(1) for CSS class application, Space: O(1)

pro_tip: In interviews, mention that Bootstrap 5 dropped jQuery dependency — this is a common misconception. Also explain when NOT to use Bootstrap: if the design is highly custom, Bootstrap's opinionated styles become fighting points. Tailwind is often better for custom designs.
